notes: Planner should stay read-only while preserving the existing email-login path and evidence expectations.

mission.objective: Implement passkeys for sign-in without breaking the existing email-login fallback.
acceptance: scope the work to apps/web and docs

workflowPolicy.excerpt: Keep routes thin. Do not auto-merge. Evidence must call out risk and rollback notes.

dryRunOutput:
## Objective understanding
Preserve the existing email-login fallback while planning passkey work scoped to apps/web and docs.

## Relevant context
The request narrows scope to apps/web and docs only, and the workflow policy expects explicit risk and rollback notes.

## Risks and unknowns
Browser support and copy changes could weaken the fallback path if the plan ignores current sign-in behavior.

## Proposed steps
1. Inspect the current sign-in flow and identify where passkeys can be introduced without changing auth provider contracts.
2. Plan the UI and docs updates inside apps/web and docs only.
3. Capture the evidence and validation expectations before handing work to the executor.

## Validation plan
List the regression checks for email login, require a test report, and note the rollback path if the passkey UX regresses.

## Handoff notes
Call out the fallback dependency, the required risk note, and the operator-facing rollback note.

notes: Planner should propose a contained control-plane investigation without mutating files or widening into GitHub integration.

mission.objective: Harden approval interrupt recovery so executor tasks do not strand after a live-turn interruption.
acceptance: keep scope to apps/control-plane and docs

workflowPolicy.excerpt: Replay events are mandatory. Preserve modular boundaries and keep routes thin.

dryRunOutput:
## Objective understanding
Plan a scoped control-plane hardening pass so approval interrupts stop stranding executor tasks.

## Relevant context
The work belongs in apps/control-plane and needs to preserve replay and evidence guarantees without widening into GitHub integration.

## Risks and unknowns
Interrupt timing, live-session ownership, and replay ordering can still produce hidden edge cases if the plan skips failure paths.

## Proposed steps
1. Review the approval, orchestrator, and runtime-codex modules that own live turn state.
2. Map the replay events and evidence outputs that must stay truthful when an interrupt lands.
3. Define a narrow implementation sequence limited to apps/control-plane and the matching docs.

## Validation plan
Require a replay trace for the interrupted path, verify the task terminal state, and document the expected evidence updates.

## Handoff notes
Keep the slice inside apps/control-plane, preserve modular boundaries, and make the risk note visible to the operator.
